rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks only into the implementation scope when binding to subcomponent ports or implementation-specific logic.
Preserve AADL component structure by using only features, subcomponents, and connections explicitly defined in the input model.
generic_rules: Use component type scope for contracts over visible ports; use implementation scope only for bindings tied to subcomponents or internal connections.
Ensure all AGREE references are drawn from the AADL model’s visible identifiers—do not invent or infer identifiers.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes use `annex agree {** ... **};` syntax with `guarantee`, `eq`, or `assign` for contracts and bindings.
Logical expressions must use AGREE types: int, real, bool—never AADL property names or unit tokens as variables.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports or internal paths in component type annexes—this breaks scope boundaries.
Do not embed AADL property names (e.g., Period, Deadline) or unit tokens (ms, Hz) as AGREE variables.
